intro: Clinopodium lepidum, also known as wild basil or bear mint, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and is native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, sore throats, and respiratory problems, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Clinopodium lepidum include volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional remedies for colds, flu, and fever relief.
